name: Phaenicophilidae
description: Phaenicophilidae is a family of passerine birds endemic to the island of Hispaniola. They include the palm tanagers and the striking white-winged and green-tailed warblers, all confined to this Caribbean island.

children.name: Phaenicophilus
children.description: A genus of two species of tanager with black crowns and pale underparts, endemic to Hispaniola.

children.name: Phaenicophilus palmarum
children.description: The Black-crowned Palm Tanager has a black crown, olive-green upperparts, and pale grey underparts. It is common across Hispaniola in forests, palms, and gardens.

children.name: Phaenicophilus poliocephalus
children.description: The Grey-crowned Palm Tanager has a grey crown, olive upperparts, and whitish underparts. It is endemic to the Tiburon Peninsula of Haiti and is threatened by deforestation.

children.name: Xenoligea
children.description: A monotypic genus of warbler-like tanager endemic to the montane forests of Hispaniola.

children.name: Xenoligea montana
children.description: The White-winged Warbler is a striking grey-and-white bird with bold white wing patches and a long tail. It inhabits montane cloud forests on Hispaniola, foraging for insects in the understory.

children.name: Microligea
children.description: A monotypic genus of small, warbler-like bird endemic to Hispaniola.

children.name: Microligea palustris
children.description: The Green-tailed Warbler is a small olive-green bird with a reddish eye, grey head, and greenish tail. It skulks in dense undergrowth and thickets in dry forests across Hispaniola.
